article_title: B.C. Reg. 74/2026 – British Columbia Securities Commission
body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 74/2026
Made under: Securities Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: May 4, 2026

B.C. Reg. 74/2026, deposited May 4, 2026, under theSECURITIES ACT[section 184]. Rule of the British Columbia Securities Commission, dated May 1, 2026. The British Columbia Securities Commission orders that, effective May 5, 2026, Multilateral Instrument 25-102Designated Benchmarks and Benchmark Administrators, B.C. Reg. 216/2021, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — B. LEONG,British Columbia Security Commission. 1	Multilateral Instrument 25-102Designated Benchmarks and Benchmark Administrators, B.C. Reg. 216/2021, is amended as set out in this Schedule. 2	Section 1 (1) is amended by repealing the definitions of"CSAE 3000","CSAE 3001","CSAE 3530","CSAE 3531","ISAE 3000","limited assurance report on compliance"and"reasonable assurance report on compliance". 3	Section 1 (1) is amended by adding the following definition: "reasonable assurance report on controls"means a report prepared on a reasonable assurance basis (a)	by a public accountant, on the statement of an individual or management of a person or company, as applicable, that (i)	relates to the description, design and implementation of policies, procedures and controls by the individual or management with respect to applicable subject requirements, and (ii)	states whether those policies, procedures and controls operated effectively over the applicable period, and (ii)	International Standards on Assurance Engagements set by the International Auditing and Assurance Standards Board, as amended from time to time;. 4	Section 1 (1) is amended in the definition of"subject requirements" (a)	by adding the following paragraph: (a.0)	paragraphs 13.1 (1) (a) and (b),, and (b)	by repealing paragraphs (c) and (d) and substituting the following: (c)	paragraphs 36 (1) (a), (b) and (c), (d)	paragraphs 37 (1) (a), (b) and (c),. 5	Section 5 (2) is amended in paragraph (b) by replacing ", a public accountant’s limited assurance report on compliance or a reasonable    assurance report on compliance" with "or a reasonable assurance report on controls". 6	Section 7 (8) is amended in paragraphs (f) and (g) by replacing ", or any public accountant’s limited assurance report on compliance or reasonable    assurance report on compliance" with "or any reasonable assurance report on controls". 7	The following section is added:

minutes_briefs.title: BC securities rules updated: benchmark reporting now requires 'reasonable assurance report on controls'
minutes_briefs.summary: The British Columbia Securities Commission has amended Multilateral Instrument 25-102, which governs designated benchmarks and benchmark administrators. The changes remove several old audit and assurance standards (like CSAE 3000 and ISAE 3000) and replace them with a new requirement: a 'reasonable assurance report on controls'. This means that instead of a limited or reasonable assurance report on compliance, administrators must now get a report from a public accountant that confirms their policies, procedures, and controls are properly designed and working effectively. The amendment took effect on May 5, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you administer a designated benchmark in BC, update your compliance reporting to use a 'reasonable assurance report on controls' instead of previous assurance reports.

key_dates.label: BC benchmark reporting rule amendment takes effect
